mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real estate financing (RSAFS) signals strong household demand for housing, which correlates with elevated consumer spending and equity market optimism, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate indicates monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant difference between easing and non-easing periods (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal labor market overheating, prompting central bank tightening that reduces equity risk appetite and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where both UNRATE and PAYEMS decline.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate weakening manufacturing activity, which correlates with lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.6M units signals robust residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when HOUST exceeds 1.6M.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures, prompting tighter monetary policy that reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive trends.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k net additions signals weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of instances when PAYEMS falls below 100k.
